zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Anne of Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk Calthorpe Drury於1460年出生在英國諾福爾可伯納姆 索普時，她的父親Sir William Calthorpe50歲，母親Elizabeth Stapleton20歲。她於大約1482年與Robert or Richard Clere結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1531年5月31日在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲享年71歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲波利 聖 艾德蒙達斯 凱德拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: Calthorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Calthorpe (Norfolk).

variants.name: Colthorpe
meanings: English:  see  Calthorpe  .

variants.name: Calthorp
meanings: English:  see  Calthorpe  .

variants.name: Calthrop
meanings: English:  see  Calthorpe  .

variants.name: Coathup
meanings: English: variant of   Calthorpe  .

nameMeanings.name: Drury
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): variant of   Drury  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Burnham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Burnham. Those in Buckinghamshire (Burnham Beeches), Norfolk (various villages), and Essex (Burnham-on-Crouch) are named with Old English burna ‘stream’ + hām ‘homestead’. In the case of Burnham-on-Sea in Somerset, however, the second element is Old English hamm ‘water meadow’, while Burnham in Lincolnshire is named from brunnum, dative plural of Old Norse brunnr ‘spring’, originally used after a preposition, i.e. ‘(at) the springs’.

History: In 1635 Robert Burnham and his two brothers came from England to Ipswich, MA, after their ship was wrecked on the coast of Maine. In the mid 18th century John Burnham and his son, also called John, were among the early settlers in what became the state of VT. In 1785, the younger John Burnham established himself at Middletown, CT.

variants.name: Burnum
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Burnham  .

variants.name: Burnam
meanings: English: variant of   Burnham  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Thorpe,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English thorp(e) ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English thorp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from any of several places called Thorp(e), which are most frequent in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and East Anglia. Compare Throop .

variants.name: Trupp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Hermann, Reinhold.

English (Herefordshire and Monmouthshire): variant of   Thorpe  , or from a place so named, such as Thrupp (Berkshire, Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire), Thrupe (Somerset), Throop (Dorset, Hampshire, Somerset), and Throope (Wiltshire).
Altered form of German   Trapp  .

variants.name: Throop
meanings: English: from Middle English throp ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English throp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from Throop in Hampshire, Throope in Wiltshire, Thrup in Oxfordshire, or any of the places called Thrupp in Berkshire, Gloucestershire, and Northamptonshire. Compare Thorpe .

variants.name: Tharpe
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Thorpe  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Thorp
meanings: Short form of   Thornton   or transferred use of the surname (originally denoting someone who lived near a large thorn bush), or a direct adoption of the vocabulary word because of its association with natural hardiness. As a given name, this is recorded in England as early as the 16th century, but the modern name appears to have arisen in 19th-century America.

variants.name: Troup
meanings: Scottish (eastern): habitational name from a place in the parish of Gamrie, near Banff. The placename is unexplained, perhaps a Pictish cognate of Welsh trwp ‘vessel, dish, basin’.
Possibly also English, a metathesized form of   Thorpe  .

variants.name: Tharp
meanings: English: variant of   Thorpe  .

nameMeanings.name: Norfolk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the county of Norfolk in East Anglia, so called from an Old English tribal name composed of the elements north ‘north’ + folc ‘people’ (in contrast to the sūthfolc of Suffolk).

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Anne of Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk Calthorpe Drury was born in 1460, in Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, England, her father, Sir William Calthorpe, was 50 and her mother, Elizabeth Stapleton, was 20. She married Robert or Richard Clere about 1482. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 31 May 1531, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71, and was buried in Bury St Edmund's Cathedral, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Bury St Edmund's Cathedral, Bury St Edmu: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈 버이 스트 에드먼드'스 캐테드럴
ko.Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Anne of Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk Calthorpe Drury(이)가 1460년 영국 노르폴크 번함 소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Calthorpe(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Stapleton(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1482년 Robert or Richard Clere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1531년 5월 31일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈 버이 스트 에드먼드'스 캐테드럴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Anne of Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk Calthorpe Drury nasceu em 1460, em Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Calthorpe, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Stapleton, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert or Richard Clere aproximadamente 1482. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 31 de maio de 1531, em Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 71 anos, e foi sepultada em Bury St Edmund's Cathedral, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Bury St Edmund's Cathedral, Bury St Edmu: бури ст эдмунд'с кэтедраль, бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England: бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Anne of Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk Calthorpe Drury родилась в 1460 г. в местности бурнам таурп, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Calthorpe было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Stapleton, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert or Richard Clere около 1482 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 31 мая 1531 г. в местности бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год и была похоронена в местности бури ст эдмунд'с кэтедраль, бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Anne of Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk Calthorpe Drury nació en 1460, en Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Calthorpe, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Stapleton, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Robert or Richard Clere aproximadamente 1482. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 31 de mayo de 1531, en Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultada en Bury St Edmund's Cathedral, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
